subject: mono parameter in util.valid_audio
truth: The `mono=` parameter in `util.valid_audio` has been removed after deprecation; code passing `mono=` will raise an error.

subject: win_length parameter in yin and pyin
truth: The `win_length` parameter in `yin` and `pyin` has been removed; code passing `win_length` to these functions will raise an error.

subject: librosa.set_fftlib
truth: `librosa.set_fftlib` is deprecated and will be removed in version 1.0; use `scipy.fft.set_backend` instead.
quote: `librosa.set_fftlib` is deprecated and will be removed in version 1.0.  Users should transition to using `scipy.fft.set_backend` instead.

subject: librosa.get_duration parameter
truth: Stop using `filename=` in `librosa.get_duration`; use `path=` instead.

associative: how to pass file to get_duration

subject: librosa.zero_crossings parameter
truth: Stop passing `threshold=None` to `librosa.zero_crossings`; it is no longer supported.

subject: librosa.midi_to_note output type
truth: `librosa.midi_to_note` (and similar notation converters) now returns `numpy.ndarray` instead of `list` when given iterable inputs.
quote: Notation conversion (e.g., `librosa.midi_to_note`) now vectorizes operations, and produces `numpy.ndarray` outputs rather than `list` when given iterable inputs.

subject: Deprecation warnings
truth: Deprecations now raise `FutureWarning` instead of `DeprecationWarning`, which may change which warnings are visible by default.

subject: positional arguments that are now keyword-only
old: passing positional arguments to functions that now require keyword-only arguments
truth: Stop passing positional arguments to functions that now require keyword-only arguments; use keyword arguments instead.
quote: Removed backward compatibility support for positional arguments that are now keyword-only.

from_fact: passing positional arguments to functions that now require keyword-only arguments

subject: official support for python 3.6
truth: Stop using Python 3.6 with librosa; upgrade to a supported Python version.

subject: audioread backend
old: using audioread as the audio loading backend
new: use a different backend (audioread is deprecated)
truth: Stop using the audioread backend for audio loading; it is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
quote: The `audioread` backend is now deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

from_fact: using audioread as the audio loading backend
use a different backend (audioread is deprecated)

subject: default sample rate conversion method
truth: The default resampling method changed from kaiser_best (resampy) to soxr_hq (soxr), which may produce different audio output quality and speed.
quote: Changed the default sample rate conversion method from `kaiser_best` (resampy) to `soxr_hq` (soxr).

subject: matplotlib minimum version requirement
truth: The display module now requires matplotlib >= 3.3; older matplotlib versions will cause import or runtime errors.
quote: Updated display module dependencies to require matplotlib >= 3.3.

subject: librosa.load
new: requires keyword arguments (0.9.0), then warns on positional (0.9.1)
truth: Most functions including librosa.load now require keyword arguments instead of positional arguments.
quote: Most functions now require keyword arguments.

from_fact: requires keyword arguments (0.9.0), then warns on positional (0.9.1)

subject: default padding mode
truth: The default padding mode for most functions including STFT is now zero-padding instead of the previous default.
quote: The default padding mode for most functions (including STFT) is now zero-padding.

subject: librosa.display.specshow
truth: librosa.display.specshow now uses centered coordinate grids instead of non-centered grids.
quote: librosa.display.specshow now uses centered coordinate grids.

subject: librosa.effects.trim and librosa.effects.split ref parameter
truth: Explicit ref values in librosa.effects.trim and librosa.effects.split now measure amplitude rather than power.
quote: Explicit ref values now measure amplitude rather than power.

subject: librosa.display.waveplot
truth: librosa.display.waveplot has been removed; use librosa.display.waveshow instead.
quote: Removed deprecated functions librosa.display.waveplot and librosa.util.example_audio_file.

subject: librosa.util.example_audio_file
truth: librosa.util.example_audio_file has been removed; use librosa.example() instead.
quote: Removed deprecated functions librosa.display.waveplot and librosa.util.example_audio_file.

subject: librosa.util.valid_audio
new: mono parameter default is now False
truth: Stop relying on valid_audio's mono parameter defaulting to True; the default is now False, so audio that is not mono will no longer raise an error by default.
quote: The `mono` parameter of `librosa.util.valid_audio` is deprecated and the default is now set to `False`.

from_fact: mono parameter default is now false

subject: librosa.filters.constant_q
truth: Stop using librosa.filters.constant_q; use librosa.filters.wavelet instead.
quote: CQT filter-bank constructors `librosa.filters.constant_q` are now deprecated in favor of `librosa.filters.wavelet`.

subject: librosa.effects.deemphasis
old: deemphasis modified the input signal in-place
new: deemphasis no longer modifies the input signal in-place
truth: Stop relying on librosa.effects.deemphasis modifying the input signal in-place; it now returns a new array without mutating the input.
quote: `librosa.effects.deemphasis` no longer modifies the input signal in-place.

from_fact: deemphasis modified the input signal in-place
deemphasis no longer modifies the input signal in-place

subject: librosa.display.waveplot
truth: Stop using librosa.display.waveplot and use librosa.display.waveshow instead.
quote: librosa.display.waveplot is now deprecated in favor of librosa.display.waveshow.

subject: Python version support
truth: Stop using Python versions older than 3.6; the library now requires Python >= 3.6.

subject: output module
truth: Stop using librosa.output; the entire output module has been removed.

subject: ifgram function
truth: Stop using librosa.ifgram; the function has been removed.

subject: amin parameter in icqt
truth: Stop passing the amin parameter to librosa.icqt; it has been removed.

subject: A440 parameter in hz_to_octs, octs_to_hz, and chroma features
old: A440 parameter in hz_to_octs, octs_to_hz, chroma
truth: Stop passing the A440 parameter to hz_to_octs, octs_to_hz, or chroma features; it has been removed.
quote: Removed deprecated `A440` parameter from `hz_to_octs`, `octs_to_hz`, and `chroma` features

from_fact: a440 parameter in hz_to_octs, octs_to_hz, chroma

subject: tuning parameter in CQT filter constructors
old: tuning= parameter in CQT filter constructors
truth: Stop passing the tuning parameter to CQT filter constructors; it has been removed.
quote: Removed deprecated `tuning=` parameter in CQT filter constructors

from_fact: tuning= parameter in cqt filter constructors

subject: util.roll_sparse function
truth: Stop using librosa.util.roll_sparse; the function has been removed.

subject: optional_jit decorator
truth: Stop using librosa.util.optional_jit; the decorator has been removed.

subject: librosa.display.specshow
truth: librosa.display.specshow now returns a QuadMesh object instead of the axes handle.
quote: `librosa.display.specshow` now returns a `QuadMesh` object, rather than the axes handle.

subject: librosa.util.frame
truth: librosa.util.frame has had its default parameters removed, so you must now explicitly provide all arguments.
quote: updates to `librosa.util.frame` to remove default parameters and relax contiguity restrictions.

subject: librosa.util.valid_audio
truth: librosa.util.valid_audio now has more stringent shape requirements, which may reject previously valid audio arrays.

subject: librosa.util.valid_audio
truth: librosa.util.valid_audio no longer requires contiguous memory layout for input arrays.

subject: librosa.feature.chroma_cqt
truth: librosa.feature.chroma_cqt now uses higher CQT resolution by default, which changes the output size and computation cost.
quote: `librosa.feature.chroma_cqt` now uses higher CQT resolution by default.

subject: librosa.display.cmap
old: returned non-binary colormaps for boolean data
new: returns binary colormaps for boolean data
truth: librosa.display.cmap now returns binary colormaps for boolean data instead of continuous colormaps.
quote: `librosa.display.cmap` now returns binary colormaps for boolean data.

from_fact: returned non-binary colormaps for boolean data
returns binary colormaps for boolean data

subject: librosa.tone
old: librosa.tone produced signals of a certain length
truth: librosa.tone now produces signals of corrected length; existing code relying on the previous (incorrect) length will break.
quote: `#1054`_ fixed the length of signals produced by `librosa.tone`.

from_fact: librosa.tone produced signals of a certain length

subject: librosa.feature.rms
old: RMS energy calculation used frame-length normalization in spectrogram-based RMS
truth: RMS energy calculation now uses corrected frame-length normalization; existing code relying on the previous RMS values will get different results.
quote: `#1041`_ fixed frame-length normalization in spectrogram-based RMS energy

from_fact: rms energy calculation used frame-length normalization in spectrogram-based rms

subject: librosa.feature.rms
old: RMS calculation had incorrect FFT normalization
truth: RMS calculation now uses corrected FFT normalization; existing code relying on the previous RMS values will get different results.
quote: `#978`_ Correct FFT normalization discrepancy in rms calculation.

from_fact: rms calculation had incorrect fft normalization

subject: librosa.core.cqt
old: Automatic tuning correction for CQT had an error
truth: Automatic tuning correction for CQT is now corrected; existing code relying on the previous (erroneous) tuning behaviour will produce different results.
quote: `#930`_ Corrected an error in automatic tuning correction for CQT.

from_fact: automatic tuning correction for cqt had an error

subject: librosa.stream
old: librosa.stream seeking behaviour on file-handle inputs
truth: librosa.stream seeking behaviour on file-handle inputs is now fixed; existing code relying on the previous (broken) seeking behaviour will break.
quote: `#942`_ Fixed seeking behavior in `librosa.stream` when operating on file-handle inputs.

from_fact: librosa.stream seeking behaviour on file-handle inputs

subject: librosa.dtw
truth: Full-sequence DTW boundary condition check is now corrected; existing code relying on the previous boundary behaviour will produce different results.
quote: `#920`_ Fixed a boundary condition check in full-sequence DTW.

subject: librosa.ifgram
truth: Stop using librosa.ifgram and use librosa.reassigned_spectrogram instead.

subject: librosa.util.roll_sparse
truth: Stop using librosa.util.roll_sparse as it has been deprecated.

subject: A440 parameter
truth: Stop using the A440= parameter and use the standardized tuning= parameter instead.
quote: `A440=` parameter has been deprecated across the library in favor of a standardized `tuning=` parameter.

subject: librosa.util.valid_audio
old: valid_audio did not enforce memory contiguity
truth: librosa.util.valid_audio now enforces memory contiguity, so non-contiguous arrays will be rejected.

from_fact: valid_audio did not enforce memory contiguity

subject: librosa.load
new: pysoundfile as default, audioread as backup
truth: librosa.load now uses pysoundfile as the default audio decoder instead of audioread, which may change supported file formats and behavior.
quote: librosa.load: Replaced the default audio decoder with pysoundfile, and only use audioread as backup.

from_fact: pysoundfile as default, audioread as backup

subject: Discrete cosine transform (DCT)
truth: Stop using librosa's discrete cosine transform; use scipy.fftpack.dct instead.
quote: Discrete cosine transform. We recommend using `scipy.fftpack.dct`

subject: delta function trim keyword
old: delta function supported the `trim` keyword argument
new: delta function no longer supports the `trim` keyword argument
truth: Stop passing the `trim` keyword argument to the `delta` function; it is no longer supported.
quote: The `delta` function no longer support the `trim` keyword argument.

from_fact: delta function supported the `trim` keyword argument
delta function no longer supports the `trim` keyword argument

subject: rmse function
truth: Stop using `rmse`; use `rms` instead.
quote: Root mean square error (`rmse`) has been renamed to `rms`.

subject: iirt default mode
old: iirt used a different default mode (not sos)
new: iirt now uses `sos` mode by default
truth: `iirt` now uses `sos` mode by default, which may change its output behaviour.

from_fact: iirt used a different default mode (not sos)
iirt now uses `sos` mode by default

subject: output module
truth: The `output` module is deprecated and will be removed in version 0.8; stop using it.
quote: The `output` module is now deprecated, and will be removed in version 0.8.

subject: librosa.feature.rmse
truth: Stop using librosa.feature.rmse; use librosa.feature.rms instead.

subject: librosa.dtw
truth: Stop using librosa.dtw and use librosa.sequence.dtw instead.

subject: librosa.fill_off_diagonal
truth: Stop using librosa.fill_off_diagonal and use librosa.util.fill_off_diagonal instead.

subject: librosa.util.match_intervals matching logic
truth: librosa.util.match_intervals now uses Jaccard similarity instead of raw intersection for matching logic, which changes which intervals are considered matching.
quote: `librosa.util.match_intervals` matching logic has changed from raw intersection to Jaccard similarity.

subject: librosa.cqt
truth: Stop using the real= parameter in librosa.cqt; it has been removed.

subject: librosa.logamplitude
truth: Stop using librosa.logamplitude; use librosa.amplitude_to_db instead.
quote: `#888`_ `librosa.ifgram` Instantaneous frequency spectrogram now correctly estimates center frequencies when using windows other than `hann`.

subject: librosa.beat.estimate_tempo
truth: Stop using librosa.beat.estimate_tempo; use librosa.beat.tempo instead.

subject: librosa.feature.rmse
truth: Stop using the n_fft= parameter in librosa.feature.rmse; it has been removed.

subject: librosa.power_to_db
truth: Stop using the ref_power= parameter in librosa.power_to_db; it has been removed.

subject: librosa.write_wav
new: write_wav no longer supports integer-typed waveforms
truth: librosa.write_wav no longer accepts integer-typed waveforms; use soundfile.write for integer output.
quote: write_wav no longer supports integer-typed waveforms. This is due to enforcing consistency with librosa.util.valid_audio checks elsewhere in the codebase. If you have existing code that requires integer-valued output, consider using soundfile.write instead.

from_fact: write_wav no longer supports integer-typed waveforms

subject: librosa.util.normalize
old: librosa.util.normalize did not preserve data type
truth: librosa.util.normalize now preserves the input data type instead of converting to float, which may change behaviour of downstream code expecting float output.

from_fact: librosa.util.normalize did not preserve data type

subject: librosa.util.frame
old: less strict validation requirements for frame
truth: librosa.util.frame now has stricter validation requirements, which may reject inputs that previously worked.
quote: refined the validation requirements for librosa.util.frame.

from_fact: less strict validation requirements for frame

subject: librosa.dtw
old: dtw was not symmetric with respect to input arguments
new: dtw is now symmetric with respect to input arguments
truth: librosa.dtw is now symmetric with respect to input arguments, which may change results if you relied on the previous asymmetric behaviour.
quote: dtw is now symmetric with respect to input arguments.

from_fact: dtw was not symmetric with respect to input arguments
dtw is now symmetric with respect to input arguments

subject: librosa.hz_to_midi
truth: librosa.hz_to_midi documentation has been corrected; if you relied on the previously documented (incorrect) behaviour, your code may be wrong.

subject: librosa.decompose.nn_filter
truth: librosa.decompose.nn_filter has improved efficiency; if you relied on its previous performance characteristics or side effects, behaviour may differ.

subject: librosa.feature.delta
truth: The trim= parameter in librosa.feature.delta is deprecated and will be removed in 0.7.0; stop using it now.

subject: librosa.feature.rmse
new: frames are now centered in the time-domain by default
truth: librosa.feature.rmse now centers frames in the time-domain by default, changing output alignment for existing code.
quote: librosa.feature.rmse now centers frames in the time-domain by default.

from_fact: frames are now centered in the time-domain by default

subject: librosa.display.specshow
truth: librosa.display.specshow now rasterizes images by default, changing the rendering output format.
quote: librosa.display.specshow now rasterizes images by default.

subject: librosa.logamplitude
truth: Stop using librosa.logamplitude; use librosa.amplitude_to_db, db_to_amplitude, power_to_db, or db_to_power instead.
quote: `#464`_ `librosa.amplitude_to_db`, `db_to_amplitude`, `power_to_db`, and `db_to_power` for amplitude conversions.  This deprecates `logamplitude`.

associative: how to convert amplitude to dB

subject: librosa.beat.estimate_tempo
truth: Stop using librosa.beat.estimate_tempo; use librosa.beat.tempo instead.

subject: librosa.output.write_wav normalization default
truth: librosa.output.write_wav no longer normalizes by default; normalization is disabled by default and only applies to floating point.
quote: `#416`_ `librosa.output.write_wav` only normalizes floating point, and normalization is disabled by default.

subject: seaborn integration
truth: Stop using seaborn integration; it has been removed and the display submodule now requires matplotlib >= 1.5.
quote: Seaborn integration has been removed, and the `display` submodule now requires matplotlib >= 1.5.

subject: display.cmap use_sns argument
truth: Stop passing use_sns to display.cmap; the argument has been removed.
quote: The `use_sns` argument has been removed from `display.cmap`

subject: default sequential colormap
new: magma is now the default sequential colormap
truth: The default sequential colormap is now magma instead of whatever was used before.
quote: `magma` is now the default sequential colormap.

from_fact: magma is now the default sequential colormap

subject: librosa.display.specshow rendering
new: specshow now uses pcolormesh and supports non-uniform axes
truth: librosa.display.specshow now plots using pcolormesh instead of the previous method, and supports non-uniform time and frequency axes.
quote: `librosa.display.specshow` now plots using `pcolormesh`, and supports non-uniform time and frequency axes.

from_fact: specshow now uses pcolormesh and supports non-uniform axes

subject: librosa.display plots coordinate system
new: plots now render in natural coordinates (time or Hz)
truth: All display plots now render in natural coordinates (e.g., time or Hz) instead of index-based coordinates.
quote: All plots can be rendered in natural coordinates (e.g., time or Hz)

from_fact: plots now render in natural coordinates (time or hz)

subject: librosa.decompose.hpss mask return type
old: hpss with mask=True returned binary masks
new: hpss with mask=True now returns soft masks
truth: librosa.decompose.hpss with mask=True now returns soft masks instead of binary masks.
quote: `librosa.decompose.hpss` with `mask=True` now returns soft masks, rather than binary masks.

from_fact: hpss with mask=true returned binary masks
hpss with mask=true now returns soft masks

subject: librosa.feature.rmse parameter
truth: Stop using n_fft in librosa.feature.rmse; use frame_length instead.

subject: librosa.cqt tuning default
old: cqt estimated tuning from signal by default
new: cqt now uses tuning=0.0 by default
truth: librosa.cqt now disables tuning estimation by default (tuning=0.0) instead of estimating from the signal.
quote: `librosa.cqt` now disables tuning estimation by default.

from_fact: cqt estimated tuning from signal by default
cqt now uses tuning=0.0 by default

subject: stft window symmetry
old: stft windows were symmetric by default
new: stft windows are now explicitly asymmetric by default
truth: stft windows are now explicitly asymmetric by default, breaking compatibility with the 0.4 series.
quote: `stft` windows are now explicitly asymmetric by default, which breaks backwards compatibility with the 0.4 series.

from_fact: stft windows were symmetric by default
stft windows are now explicitly asymmetric by default

subject: cqt output scaling
old: cqt outputs were not continuous across octave boundaries
new: cqt now returns properly scaled outputs continuous across octave boundaries
truth: cqt now returns properly scaled outputs that are continuous across octave boundaries, breaking compatibility with the 0.4 series.
quote: `cqt` now returns properly scaled outputs that are continuous across octave boundaries.  This breaks backwards compatibility with the 0.4 series.

from_fact: cqt outputs were not continuous across octave boundaries
cqt now returns properly scaled outputs continuous across octave boundaries

subject: logamplitude function
truth: Stop using logamplitude; use amplitude_to_db or power_to_db instead.
quote: `logamplitude` is deprecated in favor of `amplitude_to_db` or `power_to_db`.

subject: logamplitude ref_power parameter
truth: Stop using ref_power in logamplitude; use ref instead.
quote: The `ref_power` parameter has been renamed to `ref`.

subject: librosa.cqt resolution parameter
truth: Stop passing resolution to librosa.cqt; the parameter has been removed.

subject: librosa.cqt aggregate parameter
truth: Stop passing aggregate to librosa.cqt; the parameter has been removed.

subject: feature.chroma_cqt mode parameter
truth: Stop passing mode to feature.chroma_cqt; the parameter has been removed.

subject: onset_strength centering parameter
truth: Stop passing centering to onset_strength; the parameter has been removed.

subject: core.peak_peak function
truth: Stop using core.peak_peak; use util.peak_pick instead.

subject: core.localmax function
truth: Stop using core.localmax; use util.localmax instead.

subject: feature.sync function
truth: Stop using feature.sync; use util.sync instead.

subject: core.ifptrack function
truth: Stop using core.ifptrack; it has been removed.

subject: feature.chromagram function
truth: Stop using feature.chromagram; it has been removed.

subject: feature.logfsgram function
truth: Stop using feature.logfsgram; it has been removed.

subject: filters.logfrequency function
truth: Stop using filters.logfrequency; it has been removed.

subject: output.frames_csv function
truth: Stop using output.frames_csv; it has been removed.

subject: segment.structure_Feature class
truth: Stop using segment.structure_Feature; it has been removed.

subject: display.time_ticks function
truth: Stop using display.time_ticks; it has been removed.

subject: util.FeatureExtractor class
truth: Stop using util.FeatureExtractor; it has been removed.

subject: util.buf_to_int function
truth: Stop using util.buf_to_int; it has been removed.

subject: util.SMALL_FLOAT constant
truth: Stop using util.SMALL_FLOAT; it has been removed.

subject: librosa.display module auto-import
truth: librosa.display submodule is no longer automatically imported; you must import it explicitly.

subject: librosa.decompose.hpss
truth: Stop using power=0 in librosa.decompose.hpss; it is no longer supported.

subject: librosa.cqt and librosa.hybrid_cqt
truth: Stop using the aggregate parameter in librosa.cqt and librosa.hybrid_cqt; it is deprecated.
quote: librosa.cqt, librosa.hybrid_cqt parameter aggregate is now deprecated.

subject: librosa.display.time_ticks
truth: Stop using `fmt` and use `time_fmt` instead in `librosa.display.time_ticks`.

subject: librosa.feature.chroma_cqt
truth: Stop using `mode` and use `cqt_mode` instead in `librosa.feature.chroma_cqt`.

subject: librosa.cqt, hybrid_cqt, pseudo_cqt, librosa.filters.constant_q
truth: Stop using `resolution` and use `filter_scale` instead in `librosa.cqt`, `hybrid_cqt`, `pseudo_cqt`, and `librosa.filters.constant_q`.
quote: librosa.cqt, hybrid_cqt, pseudo_cqt, librosa.filters.constant_q: `resolution` is now `filter_scale`

subject: librosa.cqt
truth: The default `filter_scale` parameter in `librosa.cqt` changed from 2 to 1, which changes the time-frequency tradeoff and output shape.
quote: librosa.cqt default `filter_scale` parameter is now 1 instead of 2.

subject: FeatureExtractor
truth: Stop using the `FeatureExtractor` object as it has been deprecated.

subject: onset_strength parameter
truth: Stop using the 'centering' parameter in onset_strength; use 'center' instead.
quote: `onset_strength` parameter `'centering'` has been deprecated and renamed to `'center'`

subject: onset_strength output duration
old: onset_strength could return a longer sequence than the input spectrogram
new: onset_strength always trims to match the input spectrogram duration
truth: onset_strength now always trims its output to match the input spectrogram duration, so previously valid longer outputs will be silently truncated.
quote: `onset_strength` always trims to match the input spectrogram duration

from_fact: onset_strength could return a longer sequence than the input spectrogram
onset_strength always trims to match the input spectrogram duration

subject: feature.sync
truth: Stop importing sync from librosa.feature; import it from librosa.util.sync instead.
quote: Moved `feature.sync` to `librosa.util.sync` and expanded its functionality

subject: chord module
truth: Stop using librosa.chord; it has been removed entirely.

subject: chroma default start note
old: Chroma started at A by default
new: Chroma now starts at C by default
truth: Chroma features now start at C instead of A by default, shifting all chroma bin indices.
quote: Chroma now starts at `C` by default, instead of `A`

from_fact: chroma started at a by default
chroma now starts at c by default

subject: exception class
old: librosa-specific exceptions used generic Python exceptions
truth: All librosa-generated exceptions now use the new class librosa.ParameterError instead of generic exceptions.
quote: changed all librosa-generated exception to a new class librosa.ParameterError

from_fact: librosa-specific exceptions used generic python exceptions

subject: FFT backend
truth: FFT operations now use scipy.fftpack instead of numpy.fft, which may produce slightly different numerical results.
quote: switched from `np.fft` to `scipy.fftpack` for FFT operations

subject: filters.constant_q return value
old: filters.constant_q did not return filter lengths
truth: filters.constant_q now returns filter lengths in addition to previous outputs, changing the return tuple length.

from_fact: filters.constant_q did not return filter lengths

subject: util.buf_to_int
truth: Stop using librosa.util.buf_to_int; it is deprecated.

subject: output.frames_csv
truth: Stop using librosa.output.frames_csv; it is deprecated.

subject: segment.structure_feature
truth: Stop using librosa.segment.structure_feature; it is deprecated.

subject: filters.logfrequency
truth: Stop using librosa.filters.logfrequency; it is deprecated.

subject: feature.logfsgram
truth: Stop using librosa.feature.logfsgram; it is deprecated.

subject: Spectrogram frames
truth: Spectrogram frames are now centered instead of left-aligned, which changes time alignment of all STFT-based features.
quote: Spectrogram frames are now centered, rather than left-aligned.

subject: Normalization in logfsgram
truth: Normalization was removed from librosa.feature.logfsgram(), so it no longer normalizes output by default.

subject: perceptual_weighting
truth: perceptual_weighting() was moved from librosa.feature into librosa, so imports from librosa.feature will break.
quote: Moved perceptual_weighting() from librosa.feature into librosa

subject: stack_memory
truth: stack_memory() was moved from librosa.segment into librosa.feature, so imports from librosa.segment will break.
quote: Moved stack_memory() from librosa.segment into librosa.feature

subject: chromagram and logfsgram energy vs power
truth: librosa.feature.chromagram() and logfsgram() now use power instead of energy, changing output values.
quote: librosa.feature.chromagram() and logfsgram() now use power instead of energy

subject: onset_estimate_bpm
truth: Replace onset_estimate_bpm with estimate_tempo.

subject: n_fft parameter from istft
truth: Stop passing n_fft to librosa.istft() as it has been removed.

subject: librosa.mel_frequencies return count
truth: librosa.mel_frequencies now returns n_mels values instead of n_mels+2 by default.

subject: librosa.decompose.hpss default window
truth: librosa.decompose.hpss now uses a default window of 31 instead of the previous value.

subject: onset strength detrending default
old: onset de-trending was enabled by default
truth: librosa.onset.onset_strength no longer de-trends by default; you must explicitly enable it.
quote: disabled onset de-trending by default in `librosa.onset.onset_strength`

from_fact: onset de-trending was enabled by default

subject: librosa.ifgram interface
truth: librosa.ifgram has broken compatibility with ifgram.m and now matches the stft interface.

subject: librosa.decompose.hpss default power parameter
truth: librosa.decompose.hpss now uses a default power parameter of 2.0 instead of the previous value.

subject: librosa.load return dtype
truth: librosa.load() now returns single-precision (float32) audio by default instead of double-precision.

subject: librosa.output.segment_csv
truth: Replace librosa.output.segment_csv with librosa.output.frames_csv.
